item.title: The whole warehouse is gone
item.description: Ukraine has hit a major storage warehouse for the Russian version of Amazon. A major step up in the 40 days promised of increased and protracted escalation from the Ukrainian military. Slava Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Snuggetchamp

item.title: Russian military is using Wildberries (the russian version of amazon) for logistics. Ukraine took notice and removed Wildberries (in Moscow) from existence.
item.dc:creator: vladrichdemaclant

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1751 (beach episode)
item.description:
Let's get this bread.
Hell yeah
This feels @phuckedinphilly coded.
Technically a Griffinfly. But yeah, we're talking American Robin-sized
Pippin tax! Today's message from Pippin: "Glory Pippin! Thank you." ___ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 3) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 4) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride 5) Since multiple people have requested this over the last two weeks or so, my ko-fi is: https://ko-fi.com/codyandpippin
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: GovBrief Today #533
item.description: Ordinary people just cost ICE's jailers their lender. For months they protested outside Citizens Bank branches from Washington to Vermont and moved $300 million to other banks. On Friday, Citizens said it will stop lending to GEO Group and CoreCivic, the private prison companies that run ICE's biggest detention centers. The bank swears the decision was purely business, but it made that announcement one day before thousands of people planned to show up at its branches again. John Lewis called this form of protest good trouble. Three days of voter registration and civil rights organizing in his name run through the weekend at hundreds of locations. Public pressure moved a bank this week. I've emailed 8,277 stories over 533 straight nights, free and without ads. The algorithm can't show them all to you. Add your email address at govbrief.today and I will. Subscribe (It's free!): https://gbounacos.substack.com/ Today's full brief: https://go.govbrief.today/260717-full [1] https://go.govbrief.today/wildfire-tariffs [2] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-july17 [3] https://go.govbrief.today/kenya-quarantine [4] https://go.govbrief.today/taylor-tacobell [5] https://go.govbrief.today/mullin-elections [6] https://go.govbrief.today/lindell-registration [7] https://go.govbrief.today/state-grants [8] https://go.govbrief.today/nj-weapons-ban [9] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-supports-graham [10] https://go.govbrief.today/good-trouble [11] https://go.govbrief.today/pollinators [12] https://go.govbrief.today/citizens-bank Subscribe (It's free!): https://gbounacos.substack.com/
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.dc:creator: thornedrose27

item.title: Imagine how nice Florida would be
item.description: from: istorycoolkids on IG. Between 1778 and 1871, the United States ratified about 370 treaties with Native American nations. Under the U.S. Constitution, these treaties became tne supreme law of the land, recognizing tribal sovereignty and guaranteeing territory, hunting rights, and other protections. Historians widely agree that nearly all were later violated, ignored, or substantially altered through forced removals, land seizures, congressional acts, or other government actions as the United States expanded westward. n 1871, Congress ended the practice of making treaties with Native nations, although the treaties already in force remained legally binding. This map is based on treaty boundaries and later court rulings recognizing lands that were never lawfully ceded, illustrating how different the United States could have looked had those treaty obligations been fully honored.
item.dc:creator: AllAnusMorissette

item.title: So shines a good deed in a weary world.
item.description: Someone will try again, and the world will be a better place if they're successful. We will remember where we were when we got the good news, like the mirror of JFK or MLK to our generation.

Andrew Easton is Public school teacher, socialist, and father of three running for NY-15. On the ballot against Ritchie Torres in November.

item.title: The least satisfying "I told you so" in history
item.description: This, sadly, applies to many people. One imgur user in particular frequently made posts attacking Harris during the leadup to the 2024 election, and now regularly makes posts complaining about the actions of the Trump administration. This person seems to have blocked everyone who ever tried to remind them of their own words and actions. It would be a shame if this image (or better yet, one made with competence and pixels) were to flood the comments of that user's posts.
item.dc:creator: BenjyX55

Bob checking out the house

item.title: Control your dog before someone else does!

item.title: EU has forced the Mass Surveillance law through
item.description: I made a couple of posts about this a year ago, trying to get more eyes on the issue. On July 9th the EU parliament forced through a temporary version of Chat Control, the mass surveillance system scanning all private messaging. They waited until a large portion of the opposing MEPs were on vacation to force the vote. This is European democracy dying right in front of your eyes. Louis Rossmann's video on the topic: https://youtu.be/nRqqihy7l10
item.dc:creator: heyletsbefriends
